notes.anchor: he is most fiercely conducting his levy, seizing positions, garrisoning them with troops
notes.note: The levy (dilectus) was the raising of fresh legions by conscription; the report has Caesar pressing it hard even while his envoy talks peace, seizing and garrisoning towns down the Adriatic coast as he advances through Picenum.

notes.anchor: has given notice to his debtors that they are to keep the loan at the same rate of interest
notes.note: The political crisis triggered a credit panic: lenders like Q. Titinius and L. Ligus, short of ready cash, served notice (denuntiare) that they would not call in the principal but required borrowers to keep paying interest at the standing rate. This freeze is the publica difficultas Cicero blames for Quintus's inability to pay Atticus.

notes.anchor: any means of getting it in from Egnatius nor of taking up a new loan anywhere
notes.note: Taking up a new loan to discharge an old debt was versura, the standard Roman device of refinancing; with credit frozen, Quintus could neither collect what Egnatius owed him nor borrow afresh to settle his own account with Atticus.
